label: BREAKING
hook: CENTCOM REQUESTS HYPERSONIC MISSILES FOR IRAN OPERATIONS
stat1: Hypersonics travel 5x speed of sound — no defense exists yet
stat2: US-Iran talks still technically active as of this week
stat3: Last hypersonic test by US was December 2024
tts_text: Breaking. Central Command formally requests deployment of hypersonic missiles to the Middle East for possible use against Iran. This comes while US-Iran diplomatic talks remain officially open.
caption:
CENTCOM just filed a formal request to deploy hypersonic missiles to the Middle East — specifically for contingency operations against Iran.

The timing here is the story, not the hardware. US-Iran negotiations are technically still alive. State Department has maintained backchannel communication. Yet the Pentagon is moving forward with hardware designed for a conflict scenario. This isn't coordination — it's hedging.

Hypersonics are the acceleration play: five times the speed of sound, no proven air defense system exists to intercept them. The US tested its AGM-183 ARRW in December. Deployment signals CENTCOM believes diplomacy has a shelf life measured in weeks, not months.

— The Clam Letter
